review_text: The staff are all fabulous and really take good care of the park. The grounds are amazing, especially the pool. Location is great.
5 stars for Sharen who showed us nothing but kindness. She’s the best :)

Stayed on an oceanfront site at Discovery Parks Broome and it was outstanding. The sites are large and well laid out – plenty of room to park the caravan, set up the awning, and still have space around you. The view alone makes it worth it.

We had an ensuite site which was clean, spacious, and well maintained. Amenities across the park are excellent – on par with, if not better than, other parks in Broome.

We originally stayed at another caravan park near Cable Beach (three-letter name) but moved after two nights due to constant traffic noise from nearby venues and the tight, gravel sites where you’re right on top of each other. In comparison, Discovery Parks feels far more open, relaxed, and family-friendly.

The pool here is a standout – larger, cooler, and actually refreshing, with a bit of a Bali-style feel. For kids, this place is hard to beat. You’re right next to the splash park, playgrounds, and the jetty. We fished there daily and were catching trevally, cod, and queenfish, and even saw a big friendly groper. Plenty of fish life and a great experience for families.

Location-wise, it’s more central and accessible, whereas the Cable Beach park felt isolated.

Only minor downside – there is some public access nearby, so just be sensible and don’t leave valuables out. Like anywhere, opportunistic theft can happen if things are left unattended.

Overall, highly recommend Discovery Parks Broome. Great layout, great facilities, and ideal for families.

We camped in motorhome beach front and it was fabulous.  The pool was a delight, actually refrigerated, which was amazing. Only issue was that the gates were locked before dark  ie 5.30pm so after a big day out we couldn't access the pool for a cool off. The pool had lights both in the water and around table pergolas so it seemed a waste.
Toilets and showers were modern and clean but toilets only had 1 very small hand basin for 6+ toilets. Also no fans or aircon....
Location very good and staff nice. Shop didn't carry any essential lines though.

Awesome location, town beach is literally in your backyard.
The rooms are spacious and super clean. Very comfortable if you are looking to relax. Definitely recommend.

What can you say an airconditioned cabin to walk into neat clean tidy full amenities, food vans on site during the week,so you don’t have to go get dinner. Coffee van on site with awesome coffee and fresh muffins out of the oven, great staff peaceful site cabin was great.
Beautiful on the ocean with a pool, when travelling in a caravan that is luxury.

Ora at the front desk was really helpful.
Facilities are really clean.
Only drawback is the prickles in the grass. Some sites are really bad. That is the only reason I wouldn't give it 5 stars.
Bij broome vacation park slecht geholpen. Maar dit park weet wat service is ook al zijn ze dicht.zeer vriendelijke mevrouw hielp ons per telefoon wat we moesten doen perfect.
Excellent park, incredible views,and friendly, helpful staff

Seems like a terrific place to stay and work! No other park in the town is more conveniently located and oceanfront! Spectacular views from a majority of the sites. Year over year is clear to see Discovery are investing in this park and making every effort to bring it into the 21st century.
Excellent spot with great prices, and the staff are super helpful and friendly

Myself and a friend stayed a couple times in chalets. Had a very comfortable, relaxing time. Self contained with the mod cons. Also camping ground, close to shops, town beach is a stones throw, water park for kids, fishing, swimming and night market.

Don’t stay here if you wish to not be robbed!
Firstly we had 4 weeks here. We were promised shade upon booking due to our length of stay, turned up to a site with no shade at all. After much discussion with the rude receptionist they finally gave us a shady spot.
Then the thieving started! All hours of the night and early morning. My partner and I chased them out of the park many a times as we are early risers. They were even breaking into cars where the owners were adamant they locked them.
The park is well aware of this issue as they send daily texts to keep your valuables safe.
The security is absolutely dismal, all we saw were two cameras at the camp kitchen.
Never again!

Spent a week here we were suppose to be spending 2 weeks here but due to cyclone Hayley we were evacuated from the park.
We were allocated a spot on the water front which was nice.  We used the laundry facilities unfortunately the dryer was useless and everything was still quiet wet after half an hour of being in the dryer and there was no iron available.
The open  campers kitchen is good fridge bbq plates stove top oven and even an air fryer is supplied .
The single large toilet/shower combos are exceptionally clean and modern.
The camp ground are well kept some sites are just dirt like the waterfront sites the higher sites do have concrete pads
The lady in office was extremely friendly and very helpful

If you like Fishing from Shore the Jetty few minutes walk outside The park.

Yeah there is Big Sharks more in high tide.

Also you may see Turtles, Dolphins, Stingrays and more
Wir waren hier 2022 gewesen und sehr zufrieden. Tolle Lage direkt am Meer was kann es besseres geben. Haben die Sonnenuntergänge genossen. Das Personal war sehr freundlich.👍

Stayed in both beachfront sites and cabin. The BEST place we have stayed at in the Kimberley. Cleanest facilities. Great service. Perfect location for Town Beach, walk to markets and Matsos. Great water park nearby if grandchildren visiting. As with any place, just ensure you keep your property secure. (The park managers ensure that warnings are provided). Can't fault the place! We have already planned more stays there.

First time to Broome and we absolutely loved this resort! So kid friendly- great pool! Overlooked the beautiful ocean too. Very self sufficient apartment and comfy beds! Would definitely recommend and return!! ☺️

Unfortunately we were robbed the first night, most things were revived by the local police. A few were robbed that night and apparently it’s a regular thing. We felt very unsafe and couldn’t wait to leave!

We stayed at this park for four nights with a campervan ensuite. Unfortunately, the site was very uneven and full of loose sand. If you used the ensuite, you came out wetter than you went in. It has a window and a hole in the door, but the fan is far too small to keep the small space cool and dry. They use a 100mm extractor fan, which isn't suitable for this space.
The park has a beautiful swimming pool, which is wonderfully cool (28 degrees) and has a lovely view of the bay.

We booked a beach front site for 3 nights and were upgraded to an ensuite site. We looked straight out onto the magnificently turquoise water with the beach just across a strip of grass. We then needed three more nights due to car troubles but unfortunately our site was not available so we moved just two along though without an ensuite (no big deal, we have a shower and toilet in our caravan). We stayed at nearly the end of the season so not packed. One of the best located caravan parks we have stayed at.

We were lucky enough to get a waterfront site. The water is so amazing. Colour and temperature. The pool is very clean and well maintained. There is a pizza wagon that comes on a Saturday evening. Highly recommended. Shops are only 6 km away.
Well contained cabins. Park staff friendly and helpful. Just an all round great atmosphere.

We had a powered caravan site for our ten day stay. The ablutions needs updating but it was clean and functional.  It is a good size swimming pool,  nice BBQ area, camp kitchen and the views are excellent.  we didn't book a seaview site but we could see the ocean from where we were. The staff was very friendly and always willing to help.  Thanks team.
Great place to stay, a bit pricey though but still nice
Great campground, fantastic location. We had a powered site right on the water. Clean amenities, friendly staff. We didn't experience any of the things negative reviews have raised. Highly recommend.

I rang a couple weeks ago 4 times as we were coming into Broome for a caravan site and no one answered.  Left a message to ring me back but nobody did. In the end we went to another park.  The next day we got a call from discovery which was no good to us. It was off season so we were surprised they didn't want our business.
Great location on h Th he beach and day walk to town and Town Beach Markets / Staircase to the Moon. Watch the sunrise from the same location - better than the staircase to the moon in my opinion.
Convenient, spotless and very comfortable

We visited twice, with a day break in between. The first time, we stayed one night at an ensuite campsite on the first row of the beach, and the second time, we stayed three nights at an ensuite campsite on the second row. The private bathrooms are very convenient; towels for the floor and hands, as well as two rolls of toilet paper, are provided. The view is quite nice, but the downside is that you don't have it all to yourself. Anyone can walk from the public beach to the campsite. There's only a small sign indicating that access is for guests only, but no one controls access. There's no playground for children. Perhaps this is because the public playground and the water park are only a three-minute walk away, but even so, a campsite should have its own playground. The camp kitchens are well equipped with dish washing liquid and provided. Unfortunately, the entertainment program such as yoga and kids club was cancelled due to the end of the season, but there were many guests there, no signs of low or end season. In total it's okay to stay there but it's definitely room for improvement to get five stars.

We stayed 4 nights on an ensuite.
Pros:
- location
- clean amenities
Cons:
- received a timetable upon check in with activities such as yoga, kids club that did not take place
- no playground. We used the public ones
- reception staff not well trained, we asked for an upgrade being best mate members but the boy at the reception did not know what I was talking about (French backpacker?). An upgrade was definitely available (e.g site 163 remained empty throughout our stay). I came back for a couple more questions, such as an update on the bushfire on the highway but felt like the guy did not have a clue again and the colleague was busy all the time.
- open, no security gate. Lots of people walking the beach entering the park.

You will most likely get something robbed if you stay here long enough. Luckily for us it was nothing major and just food/drink but we've heard it can be a lot more!

On two separate occasions we had our stuff taken out of the communal fridge - which was always clearly labelled just as they ask.

Just make sure you lock up your valuables as anyone can just walk into this site as there is unfenced area down by the beach.

We did like the pool area and the events the site put on like a sausage sizzle.

We stayed at this campsite only one night. Our pitch was ocean view, nothing to say about this but the services and especially bathrooms and showers were not worth the price we paid: 64AUD for 2 people in Rooftop Tent. The bathrooms could be renovated and would certainly give more value to a campsite located in a great location.

Brilliant service, there are so many kids activities at the site to keep the kids occupied. The receptionist ladies are the Bees Knees and go above & beyond! They made our stay in Broome so much easier with 3 sons

Excellent location but a tad corporate. Not one of the events happening ’by the pool’ happened or if they did 🤷🏻‍♀️? That’s 6 events out of 6? Unfortunately, the way security runs anyone can enter via the public beach, Carpark or foreshore park but paying customers can’t go into reception without finding the code for the gate and leaving then going into reception then reversing the process. Sites aren’t as stacked as Lake Argyle Discovery Park and lots of variety in accommodation types. Loved the look of the beachfront powered sites with private en-suites , unfortunately all booked out.

We have been in Broome 5 weeks at 3 caravan parks all the time on a powered site. Discovery for us is definitely the best choice.
We were cautious at first given some previous reviews about crime theft in the area but after 2 weeks we feel completely safe here. (As in any location- Lock up your valuables)
The pool and gardens are well maintained.
No complaints with the amenities and in one area they have toilet & shower to yourself.
The views are amazing (we were on site 135) and location to stairway to the moon is on your doorstep as well as night markets and food trucks.
Best of all was the friendly staff, in particular a lady called Michelle. When we went to the reception Michelle went out her way to make sure we were well looked after. 10/10 to Michelle.

Booked in for 4 nights. Had arranged powered site for the first 2 then moving to a beachfront ensuite site. Paid up on check in was told to come back in 2 days to check in again. Went to check in only to be told they don’t have our change and if we want we can move tonight and again tomorrow. Weren’t going to give us a refund as that would cover the cost of our accommodation. Was not happy and only received our refund when a lady from the office came out to help us. Very poor service and the way we were treated was very disappointing.
Beautiful clean and secure
Thank you,had a awesome 17 days,is a beautiful park.Cheer's Melissa and Paul.
A wonderful location on Town Beach. We had en suite toilet/shower which was spotless. Great amenities on site. We enjoyed the pool each day and the sea view from our pitch was beautiful.
Clean, friendly,quiet would have no issue staying there again 😀

Very expensive. Although the sites are adequate.

Utilities aren't in the right position or too far away to access sullage, etc from the correct side.

Amenities are old and poorly maintained.

People setup these large shanty camps that encroach onto the road.

The staff find it necessary to play loud music from their phones while carrying out cleaning. Very unprofessional.

Won't be staying again.

The facilities at the park were great! The individual toilets and showers could have probably been done with being cleaned twice in a day, as it seemed everyone was using the same facilities. When I used the main facilities they were extremely clean, even after the whole day. The camp kitchen was great and even had an oven. The first night we came the showers were cold but had no problems after that. On the 2nd night out shoes were stolen from outside our tent. On the last night there, there were 2 children running around from around 11.30PM-2.00AM and woke us up twice. The second time we heard them talking about trying to steal bikes. Of course we called security. The children were being very loud and were obviously very comfortable in the camp grounds. We also spoke to a couple that informed us when they were asleep people had been in their cabin and robbed them. Overall, I don't think the park is doing enough, there were no signs up to notify people of consistent thefts, just sending a cryptic text everyday. We've stayed in plenty of campsites where they have had night patrol security and that should clearly be the case at Discovery Parks Broome. They had massive book gates at the entrance but you could access the whole park from the beach on the back end, so it seems they're just there to give you a false sense of security. You end up feeling unsettled and like your privacy has been breached.
What a nice place to stay.
Very clean amenities.   Waking distance to Chinatown.   Beautiful Town Beach a short walk.
Stayed in a waterfront site, so peaceful

Staying on a powered sites with plenty of grass, amenities excellent as is the bbq areas and the pool. Nightly food vans for your convenience if tired of cooking.
So close to beach and just a short walk to shops and markets, best park for value I've stayed in.

What’s the point of having locked front gates with security codes ect when the back of the park is fully open for locals to just wander in and steal your kids bike while everyone sleeps?
Beyond belief.

If you get one of the new grass sites near pool with new amenities you are lucky, watch out if you get put down in the old section, just 💩💩💩! Old disgusting amenities and far from the pool and amenities if you get lucky like us and get the furthest site down back. Will never return to this park, even once Reno’s are finished.
The 88 day overseas to visa kids were interesting to talk to. A tad high on daily rates.

This place clearly has a re occurring theft problem which has been ongoing for a while. Strongly consider if you want to risk staying here as even when being cautious to lock your valuables away even your thongs will be stolen in the night. Doesnt really matter about the rest if the campground cant address re occuring thefts and add new measures.

Don't believe the photos. We booked a deluxe oceanfront studio and ended up with something that was nice enough but absolutely nothing like the photos. We were told that there is a range of accommodation in that category and shouldn't expect what's in the photos. On top of that, after a long day we came home to rest and a pizza truck had parked directly in front of our balcony so everyone waiting for pizza was out there and we couldn't sit outside unless we wanted to be part of it. We had to sit inside with the curtains drawn. Wish we had booked somewhere else. We have stayed in holiday park cabins before and loved it. This time it feels like a cheap trick.

The park in a good location it’s just a shame about the local residents who live in the permants section. Some of there behaviour is disgraceful . Drug and alcohol abuse swearing verbally attacking holiday people . I would never go back there for a holiday. The toilets and showers grubby not to mention all the backpackers who camp in the car park near jetty who come into park and use the facilities they have the numbers .very disappointing .
Awesome.

Great location, however let down by the lack of security between the park and the car park at the jetty. Anyone can walk through the park and it would appear that some of the local indigenous women have a favourite drinking spot on the beach directly in front of the park and then walk back through the park when they are done.
Great park. Accomodation was good. Close to town and the beach for moon rising. Only downside was the backpackers working there were hard to understand language wise.
Amazing Michelle and her team could not have done enough for us. Will definitely be back to this site clean facilities nice pool and very friendly service services.
Friendly staff and very helpful.

Good park, great facilities. Extremely clean especially bathrooms & camp kitchen. Friendly office staff & good location. Felt safe & relaxed. Dump point could use some concrete around it & hand wash station but otherwise highly recommend.
Was catching up with friends, great view of the ocean at this park near town beach

Great Stay with Minor Security Concerns

Discovery Parks Broome was our first stop on a campervan journey from Broome to Perth in July/August 2025. Since our flight arrived too late to pick up the campervan, we spent the first night in a cabin—and it was excellent: spacious, clean, and well-equipped.

The next day, we moved to a powered site with a private ensuite bathroom, located in the second row from the waterfront. All facilities were in great condition and well-maintained.

We did receive daily text messages advising guests not to leave belongings outside due to recurring thefts. It seems that unauthorized individuals may access the campground via the open area near Town Beach. We also heard similar reports from other travelers staying at different campgrounds in Broome during a whale-watching cruise.

In our opinion, the management should consider investing in nighttime security patrols, especially near the Town Beach entrance.

Aside from that, we thoroughly enjoyed our time in Broome and would definitely recommend this campground.

Excellent caravan park with great amenities.
Nice pool area and next to the beach,  jetty, kids play ground and foreshore area.

Stayed there in august 2025. Great location close to markets, beach etc but unfortunately the back packers from the car park use the bathrooms at will. Steady stream of them sometimes male and female together in bathrooms. They leave a mess, don't flush toilets, mess the floor and sometimes we didn't have access to the bathrooms. They also used the bbqs and camp kitchens. Not really fair as we're paying top dollar and don't expect this. Bit of a damper on our holidays being taken for a ride. We won't be back.

After reading reviews about cold showers and queues we were a little apprehensive. We found the opposite, ablutions are first rate, our powered site had lovely views and shady trees. The park was quiet, clean and very well run. We appreciated the info page of park and town activities and proximity to cafes. Reception staff were organised with all our details ready to go. Only thing I would note as different was no front stairs to reception. You walk round the side and up the ramp. Fair value for what you get but one of our more expensive stops.

We cannot thank Michelle and her team for accomodating us, they bent over backwards for cannot say enough as to how grateful we are , accomodation is lovely clean all the amenities you need, pool area is lovely, close to Matso’s mangrove and shopping area ||Thankyou again|Georgina Badman

I would like to thank the team at Discovery Broome for fitting us in under special circumstances. We were greeted warmly at the front desk, and all staff were pleasant during the 10 days. The swimming pool is the perfect temperature. Extra activities and entertainment was outstanding!

While on our way to Broome we tried to book five nights (2 sites) we rang many times and emailed - with no response.  We decided to see if the Broome Tourist Information Centre could help. They got us two sites for five nights but could not apply our G'Day discount (which is understandable).  Upon arrival we got two sites separately and the receptionist advised that they would credit us our discounts to be used at a later date.  One of the sites could not be accessed by caravan as it had a large "ditch"" at the entrance.  Reception found us another site and located our friends next to us which was great service.
However, the "ditch"was not attended to while we were there and other people were put on this particular site???
Two months later we are still trying to contact Broome Discovery Park to find out what happened to our G'Day discount and at $99 per night this discount would have been handy.  We did contact G'Day parks (they will only communicate with you via email or message) and they advised that they couldnt do anything it was up to the park to fix this up.
We are still awaiting any sort of response to our emails and many voice mail messages sent to them.
Great location but we will not even try to book in again at this park.

This was a well located park close to Town Jetty.  However we found the caravan bays need more delineation. Some vans and cars seemed to be encroaching on others space which made it difficult to hook up and exit the park.
The biggest problem was the campers staying at the Town Jetty carpark.  Despite this apparently not being allowed, we noted some stayed for several nights.  The amenities in the caravan park have lock codes on the doors, but this did nothing to deter the outside campers.  There was a constant stream of them using the amenities, which resulted in very dirty and wet areas outside the shower, and cold water for us.
There needs to be a different form of security on the amenities so paying guests of the park are able to enjoy hot water.

Personne à l'accueil à 17h. Après un tour du camping pour trouver notre place, un gentil couple nous a aidé à la trouver, cachée derrière une tente, numéro invisible. Petite place même pas plate pour 100$...|Imposdible d'accéder aux toilettes hommes malgré le code fourni.

This was a great park and had amazing amenities for everyone to enjoy. Location was perfect with walking distance to a lot more f things near by and perfect beach views. Maybe fix up signage and site numbers would help with locating sites a bit easier.

Hi loved the park hated having toilet in shower! Bad design  move.
People use toilet with shoes full of red sand. Leave sediment in shower floor, red, dirty, annoying! As soon as your hard working cleaning staff finish water isn't completely drained. Same as after showers floor does not drain fully need "Duck board system removed daily and replaced after seemingly.
Thinking a design flaw. Need toilets separate to shower facilities and showers need tile floors nor fibreglass.

The Chalet was perfect, it was clean, suitably furnished and had everything in it that kept both of us happy.
The staff were very nice and responded immediately to a request I made to them.
The location is also beautiful and within reasonable walking distance of Chinatown.
Also just a few minutes car journey to the best beach in Australia at Cable Beach.
A fabulous few days in such a splendid location.
We enjoyed our stay here. The rooms were clean and spacious.

Spent 7 nights in the park , great location & large sites , next door to the town park & jetty , The sites near the Ocean have 8 ensuite block , the only problem is the free caravan park in The car park next door full of backpackers that stroll in everyday for a shower , they start around 6 in the morning and continue to late evening, have spoken with the other Caravaner’s everyone noticed & everyone was complaining some even spoke with reception, due to no Hot water or No showers available to use , I asked a few as they strolled in … some just kept walking… with no care factor
How did you get the code for the door , they said they got it from reception.
Not sure if Management is aware that 30 plus stroll in every day
When you are paying premium price for a site , you have to use your own van shower & toilet and others are getting it for free .
This needs to be addressed
Good secure place, reasonable facilities and excellent place to go to see the Horizontal Falls . Owners very attentive to customers needs and try to accommodate everyone.
Had a magnificent beach front site. Easy friendly check in. Although we were warned about possible thefts we had no incidents. For location and friendly lkcals i highly recommend this place.

Stayed for 6 days in the bin corner$110  had to share water and power with permanent site by entering there sites $110 a night had to que behind back packers to use the showers $110 a night to little hot water no manager on site run by backpackers $110 night, but on the bright side only two cars were stolen from the park while I was there Discovery You Need to FIX this park no good.
No security along the front.needs Attention.

Wonderful & friendly reception staff on arrival. We booked a cabin for an overnight stay and it was practical but didn’t really reflect value for money and could probably do with a good scrub. The washing machine in the cabin was useless too. Overall the location, pool and service would bring us back.
Great refurbished park with really good new cabins.

Having a beautiful and restful stay at the park. Unfortunately the park needs a bit of an overhaul. The sites are basically just dirt and prickles. The three corner jacks peace through the out door annex mat into our feet. And amazingly there is no fish cleaning area even though the park is right near a jetty and the beach. So we resorted to cleaning the fish outside our van, which was not ideal. The kids club and yoga sessions are only once or twice a week. Would love to see these offerings everyday! Pat did a fabulous job entertaining the kids for the hour at the kids club, this sort of thing should be every day in the school holiday season.
Large park, waterfront sites, well maintained, walk to Thurs night markets
So many nice rooms with potentially nice views but the trees are so overgrown you cant see the water

Rang park - no answer constant answering machine, left msg after msg! Waste of time.
Paid and booked online for 5 nights, said that we owed $2500 for a powered site! Hiccup in their system. Also if you hirer backpackers maybe have them take English lessons as they are very hard to understand. Our site 236 consisted of 1 huge tree stump and branches, along with wholes in the ground. This was a corner site opposite a permanent resident who thought he could park his truck on our site. One day he parked us in. Then every afternoon played loud music, so we couldn’t sit outside, along with the dust. All the other sites near us were concrete slabs. Yet we all paid the same money. Ammenities still have cobwebs…doesn’t take that long to clean them away. The grounds in all weren’t kept…didn’t see anyone working on them at all….at Port Smith they never stopped.
As far as security goes, to many codes when anyone can access the park down the bottom.
To many cabins, it used to be a lovely park before an organisation like yours takes it over! And charge excessive fees for a price of dirt.
You ruined our holiday.

Very very disappointed.
We booked two adjacent sites so we could catch up with and spend some time with our grandkids.
Instead we have two sites 100m apart on different levels
My site is right alongside the dump point and washing lines, it is on so much of an incline I can't get the van level on the legs . We booked months in advance.
Five star fees for I'll be star service.
Never never again

Great location easy walk into town, markets & coffee.
We had no security issues.
We were told minimum 7 days booking but saw many sites turn over less than this!
We were on site 162...right opposite dump point..and booked over 6mths ago..one of the worst sites in park!
Probably not parks fault but when did it become the norm for people to walk right through your sites? Even if you are sitting there?? Rude entitled retirees who come back year after year and think they own the place as well as overnighters!
Perhaps a row of bushes..( prickly ones) between sites would work or some barrier to deter people walking through your living area.
Pool was great!

Great park with good facilities.  No trouble with theft but we locked things away as you should. Some of the long stay grey nomads think they own the place which takes the shine off a bit.  They walked through sites on the way to the loo as they are too entitled to walk on the paths.  Didn't give a hoot when asked to respect space.

Wow. This park has done a full 360 since I visited 15 & 20 years ago.|Beautiful big grassed sites for larger set ups. Loads of beautiful established trees. Dogs allowed.|Not to mention, a trillion dollar view. |Plenty of ablutions, washing machines and brand new camp kitchens. What an absolute gem.|Still a few teething problems, such as, the reception building's path is not on the entrance side, I'm sure they are working to correct this.  It is on the pricier side but better than the other 'dogs allowed' park option (although cheaper).|This place is just gold. |Loved it!
We have a large van the site was small had to park car on the road hopefully it doesn’t get in anymore way

Seeing as we are in a motorhome we thought it would be ok to arrive and check in roughly 40mins beforehand. Went into reception and was told by the French thing behind the counter that we can’t check in until 2pm as they need to clean up the site and water it for 20 mins as it’s dry season and they do this between every departure and arrival (hmmm this is not a hotel room, it’s literally a piece of dirt/grass that needs literally nothing)….. anyway 2pm came along and seeing as everyone else had to wait there was a massive queue at reception to check in (took about 15-20 mins waiting in line) Finally got to our powered site and was surprised by the fact that it was dry as a bone, no indication of watering at all, in fact there wasn’t much grass on our site so really questioned the lies I was told originally. Apart from the terrible service, the views are beautiful and the amenities are clean and tidy. One last thing, your staff need to probably apply for an AUSTRALIAN drivers licence ( NOT OVERSEAS) if they are going to use any vehicle in your park, I literally had to jump out of the way as a staff member was speeding down the streets in the park and I was almost struck.

They don't answer there phones or messages, cleaning has alot to be desired . Backpackers were pleasant at front counter
Alot of mozzies

Such a fabulous campground with great amenities however, the housekeeping staff aren't great at their job as the restrooms were still dusty and full of cobwebs everywhere you look. Other than the cleanliness, it's a great location to stay at or a quick stopover for the night. Watching the sunset is great by the waterfront 👍👍👍 as well.
Broome is a beautiful little country town with good shops and also a historic township which we loved.

Loved our stay here , great waterfront park . Had no issues park reminding us to lock up as there can be locals that want what you have , but we didn’t hear or see  any issues . Most Cp can face issues with relaxed tourists .great camp kitchen and a good vibe
Rented a cabin for 5 days and had a wonderful experience. Great area, comfortable stay, perfect for our needs
A wonderful park close to town and nice large sites.

Upon arrival on day 1 a group of 6+ and ourselves got locked out for half an hour with reception not answering phones (everyone had tried multiple times to no avail). Day 2 hubby went through temporary fencing after they again failed to answer our phone calls to reception (we had booked online and waited at the gate for 15mins) he got accused by the male "manager" of raising his voice to the lady at reception when he simply (and polietly) raised the issue of people getting locked out, unable to contact reception as paying guests. He told my husband that the gates were in place to protect the paying guests however the rest of the area is unfenced meaning anyone can access the park via the beach, top notch safety.

Our troubles continued once we finally booked in and found the room STUNK of dog pee. The curtains and outside day bed were HEAVILY stained and the whole verandah smelt.
The only thing going for the place is the short walk and easy access to the beach. I must also add that the backpacker at reception was lovely, allowing us to check in early and held a much better attitude than her manager.

A disastrous experience. I was staying at the campground with my friends, and we were all victims of theft at different times during the week. The site is open to everyone—anyone can walk in, day or night, with no control whatsoever. There’s absolutely no security, no effective barriers, no surveillance. It’s unacceptable to have to sleep with a frying pan within reach just to feel somewhat safe.

Je me suis retrouvé face à face avec voleurs en pleine nuit , le camping n'es pas fermé, donc tout le monde peut rentrer et ainsi volé ce qu'il veut . Insécurité total !!!! Si vous voulez sérénité et tranquillité, n'allez pas là-bas .
manager médiocre sans aucune diplomatie
Super service à l'accueil.

La sécurité dans ce camping est déplorable !!
Nous avons subi 2 vols en une semaine, un de nuit et un autre en pleine journée. Faites très attention !
Le camping n’est pas fermé et donc pas sécurisé.
Nous avons rapporté ce problème à la direction mais il n’en non’t strictement rien à faire et vous répondent que c’est habituelle.

Concernant le manager il vous parlera très mal et de manière agressive…

Les seules points positifs sont la vue car vous êtes face a la mer et les demoiselles de l’accueil qui sont très agréables.

Your campsite is absolutely terrible. This is unacceptable. There are thieves around day and night — I had my speaker stolen. On top of that, you kick people out for no reason at all, while failing to provide even basic security. The gates are useless, and your customer service is a complete joke. I’m extremely disappointed.

If you  value your security  and safety, DON'T STAY HERE. Booked for over a week ,out after 3rd night. Every night bad things happen from people  being  robbed cars broken in to or waking up at 4 am to hear and see cops all around. Locals have easy acess to campground via beach or grassed path.This occurs day and night. Crime is rampant with desk management attitude cavalier at best. Not wanting to get involved. Quoting company  policy. Lots of promises from new fence to mobile night security all to late.Corporate only understand one thing the bottom line, take your business  elsewhere then they will  get the message .Mabey instead  of building  new cabins you secure the facilities  you have. To the transient corporate lackeys that run this place your obtuse attitude and incompetence made me understand  the how and why.

Youth crime terrible, in urgent need of security fencing, language barrier in office hard to understand, way over priced $100 compared to $58 in another beautiful park and staff so welcoming.Needs a lot of maintenance laundry machines out of order, toilet out of order.||Would not recommend this park to anyone.

3.5 stars. Nice outlook. Park could do with a tidy up and trees need a trim over roads.  Scraped the roof on our 3.2m motorhome.
2nd stay at the park. Still needs a tidy up or at least someone that knows how to use a whipper snipper. Presentation aside, amenities work fine, dump point is OK and they have a nice pool. Not sure about the sign driving in. 3 Stars
Very well appointed park, the cabins are great and the swimming pool a delight.

Very nice park on a great location. My car was broken into and some money was stolen, 'thankfully' nothing else like my cards etc. Other then that, the park does warn you and and its not their fault but it is something to keep in mind. Other then that the park is nice and also the staff.
The new villas are lovely with plenty of USB charging points. Lovely shower with heaps hot water.
Absolutely loved our ensuite site. Amazing views and an amazing park facilities pool camp kitchens ensuites 10 out of 10 awesome thanks we will be back again
Blankets needed to be cleaned and filter of dishwasher, just small things but otherwise nice place to stay nice office people cleaner not so much.. nice veiws i would go back

Die wunderschöne Lage direkt am Ozean veranlassten uns 3 Tage zu bleiben. Das Personal ist freundlich und hilfsbereit. Toiletten sind sehr sehr sauber. Angenehm die Lage zu Restaurants und einer Brauerei. Auch der Pool war sehr erfrischend und clean. Würden wir immer wieder besuchen.

We loved our stay here so much that we stayed for 3 weeks. Broome is awesome and this park is a good base. We enjoyed camping this close to the beach and then took a cabin as a splurge. They are new and beautiful, comfortable beds, large tvs in both lounge and main bedroom, good aircon, large modern bathroom and with a great deck that even has a comfy lounge. So while still not cheap, these are definitely much more worth the money than those at most other parks. And dog friendly to boot!
All staff was great and helpful, thanks a lot to Lilly, Ethan, Anais, Cedric, Nic and Grant for making us feel at home :)

I had the pleasure of dealing with the nicest young lady. She was extremely helpful and very curious about my situation. I don't care if it's a facade. That's what makes people come back to places, act interested and show some personality to people coming through. 10/10 will be back.

Booked a special through Wotif for a couple of nights on the same day for one of the cabins. 30 mins later I got a call from discovery park saying they had no availability for the cabin. They asked me to cancel, I said I didn’t want to cancel as it would not give me a refund this close to booking and asked if they could cancel it on their side so I wouldn’t lose my money. I put a ticket through wotif and found it hard dealing with chat bots, I ended up resorting to putting a transaction dispute through my back once the transaction went through. I later had to book somewhere else that night which was very inconvenient with a young family. At no time did the staff at Discovery offer any other options and seemed very nonchalant about the situation. I would not book at this caravan park either directly or through third party sites.
Nice beach front, wet season, so not many people here.

If you want stay safe, don’t go there. Our camper van was broken into and our bag, computer, clothes and identity papers were stolen.

The campsite has no cameras and the gate hasn't worked since February, so the "locals" come in whenever they like to steal people's things.

I very rarely give negative reviews, but this is too important to let families stay at this campsite.

Worst camping ever in the worst city in Australia.

Close, clean and beautiful cabin no complaints! Kids enjoyed the pool. Beautiful view of the ocean and great deck to relax on. Bonus aircon in every room! All facilities in the cabin you could need besides a washing machine but they have them in the caravan park.

We wanted to check-in at low season (13.11.2024). it was 4 minutes past 4 and they said it is closed since 4 minutes but 4 employees were there and talking. They said the counter is closed and therefore it would not be possible. I offered them a 100 AUD deposit cash and they could check us in the next morning. They said no and were really arrogant. I said my experience with Australian people were always very good and today was the worst I ever had in this beautiful country.|We had to look for another place and nearby RAC was also closed but Jaquie came back for us and gave us a site and we paid a cash deposit of 50 AUD and the next day she checked us in. What difference in handling a situation!|Discovery Park Broome 1- , RAC Broome 10+

We planned to stay a week ended up nearly a month was such an awesome spot. We had a waterfront ensuite and the views were stunning. Swam off the beach every day with our dog he had a great time. Only went in the pool once... out of control children made it too uncomfortable... Actually this was sometimes an issue just around the park even kids throwing sand and bark on our car!  No problems with locals or crime but we were vigilant. Loved the climate and the town highly recommend this park we did look at the others in town and no way we would stay anywhere else.
Great park, awesome beach.
Great location but a bit too squashed in and as usual, more expensive in school holidays 🤬. Save your money and stay at Broome Caravan Park outside town, they have a Huge pool and larger pitches.
Great location, friendly staff and super clean and tidy. Would recommend, bathroom for powered site was also spotlessly clean with great shower water pressure.

What a great park, having some upgrades at the moment.
We had a site  and enjoy our stay.
Would recommend.
Not real happy had the back of the broken into on our 1st night here. The locals raided the fridge for grog but only got soft drinks. Still not real happy. No cameras at all round.
Great apartment, sites looked good, close to everything & felt very safe.
Great service from Audrey and very accommodating.

My family and I were staying here in a beautiful cabin overlooking the ocean when we got some unfortunate news and had to race home to country Victoria to be with my Nan, I went to the front desk, upset and stressed to cancel the rest of our stay.
The team were amazing, the comforted me made cancelling the rest of my stay easy and put me and my family under no pressure to check out on time. They were so kind and compassionate and even went to the effort to give me a refund, even though they had no notice in there busiest period.

I am truly so greatful for the compassion and care the staff showed me and my family in such a stressful difficult time. They were so wonderful and I truly cannot thank them
Enough.

On top of that the time we did spend there was fantastic the rooms were beautiful and clean, there was plenty of activities everyday in the park and the staff were just friendly and great down to earth people.
The facilities were well maintained and the atmosphere is amazing, it’s walking distance to so many of Broome’s many attractions!

I would 100% recommend staying here, and will definitely be back!

From the bottom of myself and my family THANKYOU!

Great location, and they seem to have a few onsite services that visit the park, like food vans and entertainment. They did, however, seem to cancel these a lot, and last minute too, so don't rely on them for dinner, lunch, etc.
The location is nice but lacks security. There is no boom gate, and areas are open for the public to walk through.
It is almost impossible to get someone to answer the phones, and our travel companions had a run-in with the manager (a bit egotistical).
Facilities were clean. The pool needs more lay back chairs and some shade umbrellas.
It would be great to have a kids' play area, as it would be nice to entertain them without having to leave the park. If you are up for a hike, there is a decent playground towards the jetty.
This place could be great, but at the price you pay, it needs a little more refinement.
Really good park. Staff were very helpful when needed. Very nice amenities. The pool area is a bit hectic but the beach is right there so you can avoid it.
Good location relatively close to the beach. Easy to walk the dog and a friendly atmosphere

Went outside at midnight as feeling unwell and discovered a very large group of locals quietly ransacking the caravan park. A group literally right outside my caravan who had gone through our esky & shoe box. Yes you need to lock up belongings while at caravan parks but after travelling Aus this is the unsafest / most exposed we have felt. Something needs to be done about security here as obviously it’s a common occurrence.

Apart from this unpleasant experience the park is lovely. Great activities, pool area etc.

This amazing modem cabin has everything you need to relax on your holiday. Had to have 2 bedroom option as 1 bedroom not available. Absolutely comfortable bed with views towards the ocean from lounge. Great patio area. Great kitchen facilities including a dishwasher.
Feels like a resort.  Lots of activities, food.  Friendly staff.  Very helpful.  Love it here.

I absolutely loved my stay at Discovery Broome, it has to be one of the best, biggest and well managed parks I've ever stayed at throughout my travels.
Grant and Nikki were so helpful, going above and beyond to accommodate everyone, finding us an overflow site at their busiest time of the year.
The live nightly entertainment, pool area, modern facilities, food vans and close proximity to the beach and town make it a must see for every traveller.
We totally enjoyed our stay. Staff were very friendly and helpful. We will definitely stay again

We enjoyed our stay at Discovery Park. The cabin house was very comfortable and clean. It had everything that we needed. Great for families. The kids enjoyed sleeping on the bunk beds. The air conditioner cools very quickly which is great as Broome is very hot. The location is near Town Beach and there’s an Explorer bus stop nearby if you don’t rent a car. There’s a small swimming pool and laundry room at the park. The staff was very friendly and walked us all the way to our cabin house. Highly recommended!
Great place to stay

This is a great place to stay me and my family stayed in a cabin which was very beautiful and clean you could tell how new they were  and the shower was huge the water pressure was amazing and it was so warm the pool was good but it was a bit dirty but you could still swim in it the caravan park is easy to navigate as well. The staff were very nice as well, the balcony was great there was a big couch as well as 2 chairs there was also some sliding doors that were beautiful the hallway is a bit little so it is 1 person at a time the cabin is very well decorated and furnished the cabin that we were staying in had 2 tv’s. The Landry was good it was card operated and we had clean clothes at the end of the day it was a great comfy little place to stay would recommend going here.
Don't want to waste my time bagging them out. But our site was untidy, didn't have wifi or TV reception.

Good views, relatively safe park
But only 2 washing lines in the middle of the park but 4 washing machines??
I don’t love having to walk down the hill to use washing lines that could’ve easily been placed in a smarter spot

Stayed here in our motorhome 3-8/8. We had a great site for 3 nights before we had to move to another site that was good but not as good as the first night. The service was friendly, the sites roomy and the facilities clean and modern. We could walk to the Town beach parks, ice cream van, the museum and Matsos Bar. The hop on/off Explorer bus stop is close which takes you into town, the shopping mall and Cable Beach.  We would stay here again.

Considering this is easily the most expensive park in Australia, I would say this park is nothing special. The amenities are average, there is no camp kitchen and they could not even streamline visitors, instead making them move 4 times in 4 days. In this case the corporate greed is just putrid with no excuses. On  separate issues, the staff were nice, the town is picturesque and our neighbours - who had to move 3 in 3 - were lovely.
Best!!!!! Great location, staff, park, pool... close to the town and jetty for fishing.

Stayed 1 week on a powered site.  Great amenities and good location to beach and stores very busy sobook early.  I liked the activities they set up eg wood oven pizzas near the pool , yoga in the morning and more. However make sure you lock up your property, there was a bike stolen and a car break in while we where there. The cabins looked great too.   Overall well maintained and cleaned daily.

Highly recommend. Lovely views and fantastic pool. Staff are great and welcoming. Location is not to far to town and has lovely beach walks. We enjoyed our stay. However lower section facilities need an update and a camp kitchen there would be good too. Grass area on beachfront sites need to be dog free as every dog in park uses it as a toilet .abit gross when enjoying view and a meal
Best place to stay, great location, and sites with ocean views. Staff amazing (especially Audrey 🧡). Food trucks and regular events each day.

Extremely overpriced for a powered site, even for peak season.
Check in is very late at 2pm.
Toilet facilities very old and dated.

90 bucks a night for a van site @ peek is extortion no matter how you look at it.
Won't allow you to check in until 2pm adds to the experience. It's not a hotel room.
Check out is 10 so what happens to the park bay for those 4 hours?
Discover the harvesting of peoples bank accounts simply because you have nowhere else to go. Corporate greed.
Booked a room via expedia rang up to check what after hours check in procedure would be as we were driving up told there had been a glitch and wasn't a room available refused to refund

Discovery park. Great amenities & good location. You can easily walk to china town, the oldest outdoor movie theatre. Not far from cable beach but will need a car to get around. The studio apartment had a  beautiful outlook over the water. Close to the pool & our daughter’s accomodation. Have music at the pool & food trucks mists days. Recommend ✔️✔️
Quite expensive. Good location.
The new room was fantastic, only  issue door handle was terrible. I had to go up then down to open, and the door couldn't be latched, so I'm continually opening it

5 Stars for the view, the pool & large sites.

However, the midday check in for vans is a bit of a stretch, &  if you need to extend your stay & move sites you are required to take your caravan out of the park at 10am and return at 12pm to then repark, setup, and settle in!
For the hefty nightly price tag,  we have never experienced this inconvenience from a park in our time travelling around the country.

We’ve been traveling on the road for 5 months and have to say this is the worse spot we’ve stayed at. We didn’t know this CP was undergoing huge renovations. The entry and exit is so tight. We were put into a tiny site, everyone is packed in like sardines and there are no way near enough rubbish bins to accommodate the number of campers. Very disappointed and can now see why the ratings are soo low.
Waterfront ensuite site had spectacular views and ensuite was modern and spotless.
Very friendly staff. Convenient location. Enough space allocated for vans.
Facilities great clean.location great.security from passing locals at early hours in the am .mmm be aware.

We were booked in here for a week with our caravan. But I ended up in hospital for 10days and not allowed to travel for a week after that. So we had to extend our stay and the staff were amazing. They worked it out that we could stay on the same site, and not have to shift the van. There generosity went way beyond what we expected. We found the park well cared for and we had amazing views. Highly recommend staying here when you visit Broome. Thank you.

Have been trying to make a booking for 6 hours
In the que then just leave a message.
15 messages but no call back

Why bother!!!
What a place stay the beach front ensuites sites are amazing

We have had multiple things stolen, and now to top things off our jockey wheel has been swapped over to a broken one.
Our Webber Q has been stole. Electric scooter stolen, small gass cooker.
There is no security within the park, there is no cameras within The park, there is no security gurads. There is no fencing or gates around the park.
Beautiful view but everything else is terrible!!!!!

Definitely alot of dodgy people in and around the park!!!!

Ce camping est bien situé. Beaux emplacements proche de l'ocean, même avec vue si l'on désire. Les sanitaires, dumping point, laverie... sont très propres.
La piscine est juste incroyable avec vue sur l'ocean.
We have had an amazing 18 day stay here. The view was amazing and the park is well maintained. We had no issues with safety, but we locked everything up each night.
One of the nicest caravan parks we’ve stayed at. Had an ensuite beach front

I can’t express how much we loved our stay as this discovery holiday park - they truly went above & beyond! I had booked a cabin for my fiancé as a surprise for his 30th birthday after 2 months of camping & traveling Australia. Jason & Charlotte went the extra mile and upgraded our cabin, we arrived in to a bottle of champagne and also some goodies! The lady on reception was ever so lovely when I came to check in and the location was really great. We throughly enjoyed our time and this will be a birthday we’ll remember forever!

I’ve paid a whopping $115 a night for a powered waterfront site with ensuite for 3 nights, the most expensive site I’ve ever had in all of Australia including in the cities of Adelaide, Perth and Melbourne which by itself is ridiculous. The view was great, but every day on multiple occasions there were trespassers (mostly drunks) from town beach right next to our site. We did not feel safe. We didn’t see anyone from the campground trying to tell those trespassers to leave. Nearly all waterfront sites are empty (probably because of the ridiculous pricing) so we didn’t feel safe by the proximity to neighbours either, since we didn’t have any.
Every day we would get a text message from discovery parks that we shouldn’t forget to lock up our valuables. There was no space to safely lock away our bicycles so we had to use our ensuite for that which made showering quite challenging.
The walk into town didn’t feel safe either.
Broome town unfortunately isn’t great, I’d recommend staying at a fenced park in Cable Beach itself.

A magnificent site on the water,  flat , sone grass with shade trees.||A large park with lots of cabins. 3 amenities blocks, all with laundries (2 coin & 1 credit card) & a lovely pool. At least 1 modern camp kitchen adjacent to pool.||Office staff friendly & have a book exchange ||Camp is being extended but no issue with any noise. Our amenities block was older bit lots of cubicles & did the job.||A bit away from the city centre,  but walkable (around 2.8 kms). On tge walk you pass Town Beach park area,  Matsos brewery, the museum, & the Mangrove Hotel. ||The local tourist bus passes close by & acts like a hop on/hop off going to town & Cable Beach. Public scooters also reach site.
Beautiful view of the bay. Great spot to see the staircase to the moon with beach front sites and dog friendly.

A bit out of town but right on the Beach at Town Beach - the water is lovely at high tide but sadly not recommended for swimming due to crocodile & stinger risk.

Office staff are good - friendly & professional (sent an email with checkin info) & provided a book exchange in office.

3 amenity blocks in park, all with laundries.  A new camp kitchen is in the centre of the park adjacent to a cool swimming pool.

A significant number of cabins in park - look fairly new & very comfortable

Caravan sites are large & grassed. Ours in deluxe area was on the shoreline - beautiful (even had a turtle out front one morning).  Wide flat site with good shade. Site was dearest/nt on our trip so far but it is Broome. Other standard sites available.

Tourist bus stop close by. Walk to town beach park (parkrun) with playground,  jetty, bbq's.
Wifi didn’t work
Stayed here for 5 days in a motorhome, great location, and met some lovely people..

What can I say? One of the best locations in Broome, the most modern of facilities and will only get better when the improvements are finished. Totally caters to all travellers. Staff are friendly and on the ball with keeping the park in pristine condition.
Nice seaside places...
Absolutely stunning place to be amazing spot to call in relax enjoy the good location it is

Really aggressive behaviour of management. . Intimidating, bullying, verbally abusive . When reporting park  issues , no use calling head office and talking to regional manager or director they brush it off making you out to be the bad person .no disabled access to toilets except at central toilet block . Grounds look unkept with lots of green waste built up over a long period otherwise Awesome location. Shame it's let down by narcissistic control freaks

Great caravan park. Next to the sea. Unpowered site narrow and sometimes in the sand. Kitchen area is new. Miss equipments (microwave and fridge). Showers and toilets are clean. Beautiful and huge swimming pool.
The worst hospitality we experienced In Australia!!!!!!!!!!!

Stayed on a beachfront power site and kept getting harrased by the locals, the guy next to us got his sscooters and fishing rods stolen. Also got a rock thrown at him. Police were called in for two of the three nights we were there. They said this always happens here and there is no CCTV.

Dog friendly, a good point.
Not much ground maintenance eg watering & leaf removal.  No visible enforcement of dogs being on leash. Excellent views across the bay. Locals  drinking on beach and making a mess, council not cleaning up put a big damper on our experience.
Speed limit of 5km not enforced and there were alot of kids in the park. In Alice and Kunanurra the groundsman checked people who sped. No signals for designated walkways from top level to bottom level of town beach so people made their own way to pool and beach through other people's camp sites
Grassy powered site was in a great position. Less Trees and Bats compared to others in town. With the membership it was an exceptional deal. Would recommend!

A sensational location with a professional and friendly team looking after you. We had a personal emergency, and the park team could not have handled it better. Thank you, especially Jeff and Storm for your kindness and graciousness. Clean amenities, a stunning pool and close to everything. @Granny’s Gap Year 😊
Closest and most convenient caravan park to the CBD. Right on town beach. Great views. The pool is a real draw card.

Vervelend als je nog even contant geld wil wisselen voor de wasmachine als blijkt dat de receptie om 16 u al gesloten is. Echt veel te vroeg, er kwamen nog mensen om te overnachten maar stonden ook voor een gesloten deur, gebeld naar het nummer wat aangegeven was leverde niets op. Zijn vertrokken, is het een idee om een stukje service te verlenen door een envelop met een plaats aan te geven waar iemand kan staan.
Het gaat hier over een camping en geen hotel

Awesome park, awesome staff, awesome location, awesome pool, clean, modern amenities. Close to everything, loved our daily walks to the Jetty to watch the turtles, sharks, sea snakes, manta rays and fish. Unfortunately, the cloud cover hindered the Stairway to the moon, but this phenomenon cab be scene from the park. Booked 4 nights and stayed 11. Such a beautiful place. 😍
Great - big - next to Town Beach
Great spot 👍

I can see why this place has bad reviews! Called up, the guy was a space cadet, I had to keep asking if he was there as he wasn’t replying. $72 a night when everyone else is $45 - $60 in September. It’s very noisy, still getting renavated but they expect full price! Haha

Had a site one row back from beachfront. Gorgeous location, beautiful park with lush gardens and  lovely pool.  Plentiful clean amenities.  Great coffee cart at adjacent park.  Miriam in the office, as well as other staff, terrific, helpful and great to deal with.  Best caravan park on our two month trip.

Stayed for a week. Of all the parks, this is the best location. Can easily ride your bike into town or the brewery. Park on the other side of the park is great for kids. Amenities were clean and showers had good pressure. We were down in the bottom part of the park. Sites big and lots of room to reserve your van into (I’m 22ft and had no problems). Only negative was that I could get absolutely zero TV reception. You don’t go to watch TV but at the end of the day it is nice to sit and watch the telly.
Great location  friendly staff   clean amenities

Beautiful location, we had our van a row back from the ocean front spots, loved looking at the blue water and mangroves.
Pool facilities awesome. Great for kids, plenty of room to ride their bikes. We stayed here in August 2022 for 9 nights.
Good facilities. This park was having some upgrades.
Good size site for our 21.6 ft van. We made some good neighbours here.
The best location in Broome. Chris and Miriam now doing a stand-up job to manage things. Accommodated me at last minute. Walk or take a Beam scooter up to town, or just chill at Town Beach.
Old school feel caravan sites, modern amenities and great spot for Staircase to the Moon 🌕

Confirmed ahead of time that we would like our site to have access to wifi. Our site has no wifi or tv reception.  When I rang straight away to advise them they said oh yes that is a known issue there.  Not good enough when you are paying $85 a night for a site.
